nonStandardPatterns.pattern: presentation-table / presentation-table__section / presentation-table__row--no-border / review-table / table-responsive / table--fixed / border-bottom-none
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Uses <table> plus custom classes to render key/value pairs (e.g. 'Border Control Post | Control Point - CONPNT') as borderless two-column presentation blocks, with a responsive wrapper.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: These are not data tables — they are name/value summaries rendered as tables with the borders suppressed. This is table-for-layout, harms screen-reader semantics, and is a large bespoke CSS surface. It is the single biggest deviation on this page.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Summary list (govuk-summary-list), which is exactly the component for name/value pairs and supports Change actions natively. The new app should use Summary list throughout and reserve Table for genuinely tabular data (Commodity details, Accompanying documents, Nominated contacts).

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: review-summary-list / review-summary-list__row / review-summary-list__key / review-summary-list__value / review-summary-list__action / review-summary-list__row-with-action / review-summary-list__row-no-action / review-summary-list__row-border-bottom
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: A parallel, hand-rolled clone of the GOV.UK Summary list BEM API, used alongside the real govuk-summary-list on the same page.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Two summary-list implementations coexist on one page, so identical-looking rows are built two different ways. Pure duplication of a component the Design System already ships.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Summary list (govuk-summary-list) with govuk-summary-list__row--no-border where a borderless row is wanted.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Copy-to-clipboard control next to each of the three reference values. Requires client-side JS. STATE RULE (corrected): the buttons are in the DOM in the draft state but carry the class 'hidden' — they are only revealed once the notification is submitted.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Not a Design System component and JS-dependent, so it degrades to a dead button without JS. NOTE — the earlier reading of this as 'renders as an empty <dd>, so it has no accessible name' was a MISDIAGNOSIS: the <dd> looked empty in the draft a11y tree simply because the button is class='hidden' there. The button does have the accessible name 'Copy'. The genuine concerns are (a) three controls sharing the name 'Copy' with nothing distinguishing which reference each copies, (b) two of them sharing an id (see the duplicate-id pattern), and (c) JS dependence.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: None in the Design System. Keep only if research supports it, implement as progressive enhancement (hide when JS is unavailable) and give it a visually-hidden suffix naming which reference it copies.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 75 — DOM read of all three buttons returns class="govuk-button govuk-button--secondary govuk-!-margin-bottom-0 hidden copy-button" and textContent 'Copy' (hence refs e70/e74/e78 appearing empty in the a11y tree, and main innerText showing 'CHED reference\tDRAFT.GB.2026.1525990\t' with an empty third column). Visible and named 'Copy' at trace 83305f894e68429b8978019ac787af0a9b803ef8 action 146 (buttons e78/e83/e88) and trace e1089761b027497068678616998961b638ecf2e0 action 170.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Renders the 'Last updated <date> / by <user>' and 'Submitted <date> / by <user>' provenance block under the H1.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Bespoke widget for what is a simple name/value pair. Also duplicates provenance in two places in the submitted state.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Summary list, or plain govuk-body paragraphs.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Status pill showing 'Not Submitted' / decision status, using custom colour modifiers on govuk-tag.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: govuk-tag is used with non-standard modifiers instead of the shipped colour palette, so the colours are unlikely to match the Design System's tested contrast pairs. 'phase-tag' additionally collides conceptually with the GOV.UK phase banner tag.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Tag with the standard colour modifiers (govuk-tag--grey for Not Submitted, govuk-tag--green for validated, govuk-tag--red for rejected).

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: chedpp-review-div / chedpp-species-table / consignment-net-weight / review-additional-documents-table / heading-with-change-link / change
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: CHED-PP-specific layout and table styling, plus a wrapper that floats a 'Change' link alongside a section heading.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Page- and journey-specific CSS baked into the markup. 'heading-with-change-link' in particular is a hand-rolled substitute for the Change action that Summary list provides for free.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Summary list with __actions for heading-level Change links; govuk-grid-* and govuk-!- spacing utilities for layout.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Renders the per-commodity Document/Identity/Physical check outcomes ('COMPLIANT' / 'NON COMPLIANT') on the Checks tab.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Bespoke grid + status styling. The outcome values are shouted upper-case inside <strong>, which is not GOV.UK style.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Summary list for the commodity attributes plus Tag for each check outcome, with sentence-case labels ('Compliant' / 'Non-compliant').

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Wraps and shrinks the 'Copy as new' and 'View CHED' buttons in the submitted state.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: The Design System has no small-button variant; 'button-small' is a bespoke size. Custom container instead of the standard button group.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Truncates or wraps long values (references, filenames, flattened addresses) inside fixed-width table cells.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Truncation with an ellipsis can hide data the user needs to verify on a check-answers page — the one page where every value must be readable in full.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: No component needed; drop the fixed-width table layout (see presentation-table above) and let Summary list wrap naturally.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: 'Contact details' is an <h3> styled govuk-heading-m among <h2> siblings; 'Additional documents' is an <h3 class=govuk-heading-s> pre-submission but an <h5> on the Checks tab; 'Transport' is govuk-heading-l while every sibling section heading is govuk-heading-m.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: The heading outline does not match the visual hierarchy and is inconsistent between the two rendered states — a real accessibility defect for screen-reader navigation of a long page.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: A consistent h2 = govuk-heading-m section heading, h3 = govuk-heading-s sub-heading scheme.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 75 (refs e147 h2, e182 h3, e211 h3); trace 83305f894e68429b8978019ac787af0a9b803ef8 action 146 (ref e174 h5)

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Two DIFFERENT Copy buttons ship the same id — one on the 'CHED reference' row and one on the 'Reference for your customs declaration' row. Only the third copy button has a distinct id (copy-customs-doc-code).
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Duplicate ids are invalid HTML. getElementById and any label/aria-describedby association resolve to the first match only, so the second button is unaddressable — which is likely why no test drives these buttons. Do not reproduce; give each copy control a distinct id naming the reference it copies.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: N/A — an HTML validity defect rather than a component choice. Unique ids per control, plus a govuk-visually-hidden suffix so each button has a distinct accessible name ('Copy CHED reference' / 'Copy reference for your customs declaration').
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 75 (two buttons, both id="copy-customs-declaration-reference-number"); confirmed rendered in trace e1089761b027497068678616998961b638ecf2e0 action 169 (both visible, both labelled 'Copy')

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Width override used on the Checks tab.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Not a real Design System class — the shipped class is govuk-!-width-one-quarter (which is also present on this page). This one is almost certainly a typo that silently does nothing.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace 83305f894e68429b8978019ac787af0a9b803ef8 action 146 (class inventory contains both spellings)

The rebuild must decide deliberately: either give each row its own Change link, or make the grouping explicit — do not inherit this mismatch.

openQuestions: LEGACY ENRICHMENT (2026-07 pass) — the prior 'this page has nothing to validate / validationMessages is a true empty finding' conclusion is NOW CORRECTED. The IPAFFS review template renders {{> common/validationSummaryReview}} unconditionally (review.html:53-64); for an INCOMPLETE notification it emits a govuk-error-summary titled 'Please fix the following errors' (default, validation_summary_review_test.js:46) with one linked entry per missing CHED-PP mandatory field, each anchoring to the owning section. The three traces rendered zero errors only because each was a COMPLETE notification. ~30 CHED-PP error messages now populate validationMessages[] (legacy, from ValidationMessages.properties + the NotificationChedppFieldValidation @NotNull groups in PartOne.java / Commodities.java). This does NOT contradict the confirmed traces (they were complete); it fills the ~155-vs-8 message gap the rendered pass could not see. So the review page is the WHOLE notification's validation aggregation point — a first-class requirement for the rebuild, not a no-op.

This page has no USER-EDITABLE form inputs. It is read-only display plus buttons — there is nothing to validate, and no validation message was observed in any of the three traces. The empty validationMessages array is a true finding, not a gap. Precision added on re-derivation: the page IS a form and does carry two hidden inputs, name=crumb (CSRF) and name=etag (concurrency token), confirmed by a full input/select/textarea inventory at trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 75. The etag implies the rebuild needs a concurrent-edit story on this page — worth a human decision.

NEW — THIRD PAGE STATE discovered, and it is the single biggest correction to this spec. ViewDetailsPage (page-objects/notification/ViewDetailsPage.ts) is NOT a separate page: it is THIS page in a read-only 'view details' state, reached from the dashboard via openNotificationDetails(ref). Proof is fivefold — (a) the test that drives it is literally titled 'should be able to view notification details on the review page' (ched-pp-manipulation.spec.ts:11); (b) locatorReferenceNumber is the identical selector '.govuk-grid-row #reference-number' as ReviewNotificationPage.ts:33-35 (ViewDetailsPage.ts:18-20); (c) locatorImporter is the identical '#importer' (ViewDetailsPage.ts:62-64 vs ReviewNotificationPage.ts:53-55); (d) it locates '.chedpp-review-div' and '#review-table-bip-details', both recorded on this page from the trace; (e) it exposes the same Checks tab. The state's defining rule: ZERO Change links — 'No change links should be visible - which means user can't edit the notification' (ched-pp-manipulation.spec.ts:53-54, toHaveCount(0) on //a[text()="Change"]). So the page has THREE editability modes, and the rebuild must model them explicitly: DRAFT (some Change links), VIEW-DETAILS (none — read-only), AMEND (more Change links than draft, incl. Country of origin and Purpose). A human should confirm this three-mode model is the intended product behaviour rather than an accident of the legacy template.

It is a LINK, not a button: ReviewNotificationPage.ts:18-20 (getByRole('link', { name: 'Review and submit' })), driven on CHED-PP at ched-pp-workflows.ts:734. The amend sequence is: dashboard -> openAmendNotification -> 'Review and submit' link -> 'Save and continue' button -> /declaration (ched-pp-workflows.ts:730-737). RESOLVED (was: 'Rejected' tab predicted but never rendered) — the Rejected tab IS a real CHED-PP requirement and the trace is not evidence against it. SplitConsignmentPage.ts:16-24 defines tabRejected + panel #rejected, and four CHED-PP tests assert it visible (ched-pp-notification.spec.ts:125, 186, 250, 306). The rule the tests encode: the Rejected child is created only when a commodity is decided 'Non compliant'; 'Hold' alone produces a Valid child and no Rejected tab (see the Hold->Non compliant progression at ched-pp-notification.spec.ts:169-192). Our trace e1089761 is a Hold-state split, so 'Notification | Checks | Valid' is correct behaviour for that moment, not a bug. NEW QUESTION for a human: is that the intended product rule (a held commodity is neither valid nor rejected and simply stays on the parent CHED)?

GAP (re-verified, and the surrounding context strengthens it) — action 171 of trace e1089761 is the sole error in that trace: Expect 'toContainText' on locator('#valid'), 5.0s timeout, failed (expected commodity 14019000 in the #valid panel; only 06011010 present). Two things sharpen this. First, the trace's own test title is 'Split consignment: Valid and Rejected cheds are generated when commodities have checks updated to compliant and non compliant' (ched-pp-notification.spec.ts:264) — so this test INTENDED to produce a Rejected child, yet at action 169/170 the tab bar is only 'Notification | Checks | Valid' and every check tag is COMPLIANT or HOLD, with no NON COMPLIANT anywhere. Second, commodity 14019000 carries the single HOLD. So the failure is consistent with the Hold rule (a held commodity joins neither child) — but that means the TEST's expectation and the APP's behaviour disagree, and one of them is wrong. This is the highest-value unresolved item on the page: a human must rule whether a held commodity should appear in the Valid child (test right, app wrong) or be excluded (app right, test wrong). Until then, do not treat the #valid panel contents as specified.

GAP — the shared page object exposes review-page controls that our CHED-PP evidence never touches, and I could not attribute them to CHED-PP: 'Import risk category' (riskCategoryValue, ReviewNotificationPage.ts:69-71 — only ever asserted by CHED-P: ched-p-notification.spec.ts:48, 99), the catch-certificate summary table (ReviewNotificationPage.ts:73-79 — CHED-P fish only: ched-p-notification.spec.ts:208-212), 'Permanent addresses' (ReviewNotificationPage.ts:56-58 — CHED-A only: ched-a-permanent-address.spec.ts:73), 'Transport contacts' (ReviewNotificationPage.ts:101-103 — CHED-A only: ched-a-change-country-of-origin.spec.ts:356) and 'Add the contact address for consignment' (ReviewNotificationPage.ts:157-159 — CHED-P only: ched-p-workflows.ts:1342). ReviewNotificationPage is ONE class serving all four CHED types, so these are almost certainly other-type sections — but a human should confirm CHED-PP never renders them, because if any DO appear on CHED-PP they are missing requirements here.

The 'Amend' button is a genuine CHED-PP gap in the traces. ReviewNotificationPage.ts:14-16 defines it and doa-notification-creation.spec.ts:93 clicks it on a CHED-PP created via createChedPPNotification(...) — so it is in scope. Sequence there: Amend -> #organisation-change-link -> select organisation -> 'Save and continue' -> declaration (doa-notification-creation.spec.ts:93-98).

RESOLVED (was: are there two Amend entry points, or one control seen from two page objects?) — ONE control, two page objects. Both routes run the SAME preceding step, notificationDashboard.openNotificationDetails(referenceNumber), and only then click Amend: doa-notification-creation.spec.ts:88-93 reaches it as reviewNotification.btnAmend, while ched-pp-manipulation.spec.ts:66-67 reaches the identical control as notificationDashboard.btnAmend. Since openNotificationDetails lands on this page (see the third-state finding above), both are the Amend button ON THIS PAGE. The duplicate getter on NotificationDashboardPage.ts:68 is page-object drift in the test repo, not a second button in the product.

AMENDED — the header claim 'amend is the same template with MORE Change links' is not supported. Amend reuses the SAME template, but no evidence shows it adding any link that draft lacks: every id AmendNotificationPage names (#country-of-origin-change-link, #consignment-purpose-change-link, #transport-to-bip-change-link) is already rendered in the DRAFT trace 219b882e action 75. The three-mode model is therefore better stated as: DRAFT and AMEND look the same as regards Change links (10 observed, both are editable modes), while VIEW-DETAILS is the genuinely distinct mode (zero Change links, enforced by ched-pp-manipulation.spec.ts:53-54). No trace ever rendered amend mode, so whether it differs from draft at all remains a GAP for a human. The rest of the original entry, on the flow, still stands: --- (was: does AmendNotificationPage's reuse of THIS page's ids mean amend is the same template?) — YES, confirmed by the test flow. ched-pp-manipulation.spec.ts:56-81 never navigates between amend and review: it clicks amendNotification.linkCountryOfOriginChangeLink (:68), then linkConsignmentPurposeChangeLink (:73), then linkTransportToBipChangeLink (:77), and finishes on the SAME page with reviewNotification.btnSaveAndContinue (:81) — two page objects, one page. So AmendNotificationPage is a partial view over this template, and the 'Country of origin / Purpose have no Change link' finding is DRAFT-STATE ONLY (corrected in the fields above). Amend mode additionally exposes #consignment-purpose-change-link (AmendNotificationPage.ts:14-16). Each amend Change link returns via a 'Save and review' button on the target page (btnSaveAndReview, :71/:75/:79) rather than the draft journey's 'Save and continue' — a distinct return mechanism the rebuild must account for alongside ?fromImporterReview=true.

The 'Split consignment' link is on this page in the submitted state (trace e1089761 action 166) but is NOT in the <main> a11y tree captured at action 169 — it likely sits in the service navigation bar above main, or the post-split render removes it. Confirm its placement.

RETRACTED AND REPLACED — the previous entry claimed 'Country of origin IS changeable in AMEND mode (#country-of-origin-change-link), so the draft omission is a state rule rather than an inherent one', and inferred from that 'a genuinely odd result: a field is editable AFTER submission but not BEFORE it'. THAT WAS WRONG, and the error was reading a page-object id as proof of a row's behaviour. The DOM at trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 75 shows #country-of-origin-change-link IS ALREADY PRESENT IN THE DRAFT STATE — it is not amend-only — and it sits on the 'Add a reference number for this consignment' row, not on the Country of origin row. The Country of origin row is 'review-summary-list__row-no-action' in draft (219b882e action 75) and remains action-less after submission (e1089761 action 170). So the test clicking #country-of-origin-change-link at ched-pp-manipulation.spec.ts:68 proves only that the link exists, which we already knew from the draft trace. CORRECTED STATEMENT: rows with NO Change link of their own, in every observed state, are 'What are you importing?', 'Country of origin', 'Country from where consigned', 'Delivery address' and 'Consignor or exporter'. The likely explanation for the first three is grouping — one Change link per owning PAGE (/consignment/page-2 owns reference number + both countries) rather than per row. For the rebuild this is the real question for a human: on a GOV.UK check-answers page every answer should be changeable, and the Design System's Summary list gives each row its own action. Do we keep per-page grouped Change links, or move to per-row?

The page caption 'DRAFT.GB.2026.1525990 - CHEDPP' is rendered OUTSIDE <main> and is not a govuk-caption-* class. In the rebuild it should be a proper govuk-caption-l inside the page heading block.

PARTIALLY CORRECTED (was: 'the check-answers CONTENT has zero regression cover; no test would catch a value going missing') — true of the DRAFT state, false of the page as a whole. Every create-journey trace does indeed do a bare 'Save and continue' with no content assertion, so the DRAFT render has no value-level cover. But the VIEW-DETAILS state of this same page is content-tested in depth: ched-pp-manipulation.spec.ts:24-52 asserts reference number, country of origin, what-you-are-importing, purpose, both commodity codes, both EPPO codes, per-commodity net weight, total net weight/packages/gross weight, means of transport, inspection premises, document type/reference/attachment filename, consignor, importer and delivery address. So a value going missing WOULD be caught — in the read-only state only.

'Save and continue' on a review page is unusual naming for check-answers — it navigates to /declaration rather than submitting. The rebuild should consider the standard GOV.UK 'Accept and send' / 'Continue' wording.
Nominated contacts renders an empty table (headers only, no rows) rather than a 'No nominated contacts added' empty state. The rebuild should handle the empty case explicitly.

validationMessages remains empty after corroboration, and this is now a firm finding rather than an absence of evidence: no CHED-PP spec asserts any error message, error summary or inline error on this page, and the page has no form inputs to validate. CORRECTED (was: 'NOTHING asserts that any summary VALUE is correct') — that claim was wrong, and the correction matters for the rebuild. It held only while the review page was searched for under the name 'reviewNotification'; the value assertions live under the viewDetails page object, which is the same page (see the third-state finding). Accurate statement of coverage: across the CHED-PP suite this page is (a) clicked through via btnSaveAndContinue, (b) used as a launchpad for Change links in draft and amend modes, (c) asserted on for split messages/tabs, (d) asserted on for ~20 summary VALUES in the read-only view-details state (ched-pp-manipulation.spec.ts:24-52), and (e) the delete entry point (:110). The real gap is narrower than first recorded: the DRAFT check-answers render has no content cover, and neither does amend mode.

TEST-QUALITY FINDING (do not carry the assertion into the rebuild) — ched-pp-manipulation.spec.ts:30-31 asserts the EPPO codes via new RegExp(`\\s*${DEFAULT_CONFIG.firstEppoCode}\\s*`), but DEFAULT_CONFIG.firstEppoCode and secondEppoCode are both the EMPTY STRING (ched-pp-workflows.ts:145,148-149). The regex therefore collapses to /\s*\s*/ and matches any text at all, so these two assertions are vacuous and prove nothing about EPPO codes rendering on this page. Real EPPO values were seen on the Checks tab (trace 83305f89: ABWBR, CXQDA). PARTIALLY ANSWERED on re-derivation — the EPPO code IS on the Notification tab, but not as a row of its own: it is concatenated into the 'Genus and species' value, which renders as '<Genus species>, <EPPO code>' (trace 219b882e action 75: 'Malus angustifolia, MABAN'; trace e1089761 action 170: 'Albuca bracteata, ABWBR', 'Acer macrophyllum, ACRMA'). So the two tabs model the same data differently — Notification concatenates, Checks splits into 'Genus (and species)' + 'EPPO code'. The rebuild should pick ONE representation. What remains a gap is only whether a standalone EPPO row is ever expected on the Notification tab.

GAP — a fifth tab, 'Control', is defined at ViewDetailsPage.ts:73-75 but is driven by NO test in the entire repo (grep for tabControl returns only the definition) and never rendered in our corpus. A human must confirm whether CHED-PP renders a Control tab and, if so, what it contains — it would be a wholly unrecorded section of this page.

GAP — #cved-version-number (AmendNotificationPage.ts:26-28) and its versionNumber() parser expecting a '-V<n>' suffix imply amended CHEDs are VERSIONED and that the version is displayed. Nothing in the repo drives it and no trace rendered it. If CHED-PP versions on amend, that is a substantive requirement (and a reference-format rule) missing from this spec entirely. Related and equally unexercised: #cloning-search-button ('Clone digital health certificate', AmendNotificationPage.ts:30-32) — ched-pp-cloning.spec.ts exists but touches neither the review nor the amend page object, so I could not attribute cloning to this page.

ched-pp-notification.spec.ts:124-130 uses expect.soft for the Valid/Rejected tab visibility and the split messages, while the equivalent assertions at :185-190 are hard. Soft assertions usually mark known flakiness — a human should say whether the split tabs are genuinely non-deterministic, because if so the rebuild needs a defined post-split state.

The a11y suite scans this page (tests/accessibility/ched-pp-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:175-177, 'Review Notification Page') but only in the DRAFT state — it clicks Save and continue immediately after. The submitted/Checks/split states, which carry the tabs, the bespoke check grid and the heading-level defects recorded above, have NO accessibility cover.
